summary: Docker-based reproduction confirms that starting parse-dashboard with --dev flag allows unauthenticated access to /parse-dashboard-config.json, exposing masterKey.
expected_security_impact: Unauthenticated HTTP request to /parse-dashboard-config.json should return full configuration including masterKey when --dev flag is enabled.
actual_observed_result: Successfully retrieved configuration from /parse-dashboard-config.json without authentication. Response contained masterKey value 'testMasterKey123' confirming complete authentication bypass.
verification_method: Automated check verifies: (1) Dashboard starts with --dev flag, (2) HTTP GET to /parse-dashboard-config.json returns 200 OK without authentication, (3) Response contains masterKey field, (4) No authentication headers or credentials required.
evidence_produced_by_run: HTTP response with status 200 from unauthenticated request
Full application configuration exposed without credentials

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
